free_text: Analyzing powers and spin correlation coefficients
for p+d elastic scattering at 135 and 200 MeV
polarized proton beam in the Indiana
Cooler synchrotron
Protons are produced by a polarized ion source,
accumulated in the injector synchrotron and then
injected into the Cooler. The beam polarization is
typically 0.75.
The internal, polarized deuteron target is generated by
injecting polarized atoms from an atomic beam source
(ABS) into a storage cell. The target cell is a 27 cm
long tube of 12mm diameter made from 0.05 mm thick
aluminum
The forward going particle is detected in a
stack consisting of a dE detector, two wire chambers
with two wire planes each, and a stopping detector.
The recoil particle is detected in a so-called
silicon barrel that consists of an array of 18
silicon strip detectors surrounding the target cell.
Data received from main author on 3/15/2007
